title: IP Addressing, DNS, DHCP, NAT, VLAN, and VPN · Mastery Check
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for ip addressing, dns, dhcp, nat, vlan, and vpn and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layered IP configuration and dependency trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret IPv4 and IPv6 address, prefix, host, subnet, gateway, loopback, link-local, private, public, dynamic, and static roles.
Trace discovery, lease, reservation, exclusion, scope, options, renewal, relay, conflict, fallback, and authoritative ownership.
Trace local names, hosts data, client cache, configured resolver, recursive and authoritative answers, record types, TTL, search suffixes, and application caching.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate tunnel establishment, identity, certificate, routes, DNS, split policy, address overlap, filtering, application state, and privacy limits.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Vpn Evidence, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Vpn Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Vpn Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Duplicate addresses, stale names, a guest isolation leak, an unreachable subnet, and VPN split behavior are all blamed on DNS. The quiz variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate tunnel establishment, identity, certificate, routes, DNS, split policy, address overlap, filtering, application state, and privacy limits.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Vpn Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A connected VPN status means all intended traffic is protected and reachable.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using separate and tunnel plus specific case evidence.
